feed.title: SAP Community - ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model
feed.subtitle: ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: EML Get Permission from SAP BO ( Billing )
entry.content:
Hello,

I have developed a custom app on the public cloud ( developer extensibility ).

I use the I_BILLINGDOCUMENTTP-CreateFromExternalData action (SAP Business Accelerator Hub - I_BILLINGDOCUMENTTP)
Furthermore I have set up two roles, one being able to execute the function via the Sales-Billing Document catalog (user "write"), the other being only able to create data in my app but not trigger the function above (user "read").
(Billing Document | SAP Help Portal)
If the read user performs the action, the eml fails and i can catch the error. (so far so good)
My intention now is to check if he is allowed to use the action via the global features method and disable the button if he is not.

I know that there is the GET PERMISSION ONLY GLOBAL AUTHORIZATION .... feature, but it returns true for all inputs on my end.
If I check the SAP implementation of I_BillingDocument~CreateFromExternalData, i can see, that the global authtorization method is not implemented and instead works with authorization context.
I can see there and in the business catalog for "write" (SAP_BCR_SD_BIL_DOC_PC) the required authorization objects.
Some of the authorization objects are not cloud ready and therefore cannot be used.

So my question is:
How do I check if the user can use the CreateFromExternalData action without using it in the first place?

entry.title: Verify RAP Compatibility on SAP S/4HANA 2020 On-Premise?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I would like to confirm whether my SAP landscape supports SAP RAP (RESTful Application Programming Model) development and deployment.

Current system details:

  • SAP S/4HANA On-Premise 2020

  • Database: SAP HANA 2.0 (Release 2.00.086.00.1751264911)

  • SAP Logon 760

  • Development Tool: ABAP Development Tools (ADT) in Eclipse

I would like to know:

  1. Does SAP S/4HANA On-Premise 2020 support RAP development and are managed and unmanaged RAP scenarios both supported in this release?

  2. Can RAP applications be developed, deployed, and consumed as SAP Fiori applications directly from this system?

  3. Are there any minimum ADT, SAP_BASIS, SAP_ABA, or backend component requirements that should be verified before starting RAP development?

  4. Is there a recommended transaction, SAP Note, or system check to confirm RAP readiness in the backend?

Additionally, what is the recommended approach to verify RAP compatibility on an on-premise system?

Any guidance on prerequisites, limitations, compatibility checks, or experiences with RAP on S/4HANA 2020 would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in Advance.

Research and Development  SAP Enterprise Support SAP S/4HANA for product compliance

entry.title: Call Hana trial ABAP V4 RAP from Vscode / postman
entry.content:
I created a custom RAP V4 service in Hana trial and published it.

When calling from vscode / postman i provided the client credentials from the ABAP service(key etc.).

in Vscode i provided below details for the first call to get token.  

Auth token url

client id 

client secret

when i do get token it gets the token successfully. 

Then next call to call service metadata gives 200 but returns the login page.

 

Any clue what needs to be fixed ?

entry.title: Extending S/4HANA Standard Event Header with SalesOrderType (AUART) for Event Mesh routing
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I am working on an event-driven architecture using SAP S/4HANA and SAP Advanced Event Mesh and need some guidance on event extensibility -- unfortunately I did not find many information.

My business requirement is the following: "I want to implement event routing based on the sales order type (AUART) and the consumer destination, so that each system receives only the statuses applicable to its scope."

To achieve this via AEM topic subscriptions (wildcards), I need the SalesOrderType / AUART field to be present in the Header / Topic, not just inside the data payload.

  1. I attempted to extend the standard event (sap.s4.beh.salesorder.v1.SalesOrder.Created.v1) using a Metadata Extension to apply the @event.context.attribute annotation. However, the underlying standard RAP BO (R_SALESORDERTP / D_SalesOrderCreated) does not have the @Metadata.allowExtensions: true annotation, which blocks this approach.
  2. I also considered creating a custom RAP Event from scratch, but I've failed so far and did not find a good tutorial to help me on this.

My Questions:

  1. What is the recommended Clean Core compliant approach to promote a payload field (SalesOrderType) into the Header?

  2. Is it recommended to use BAdIs to intercept and enrich the header dynamically before it is dispatched? Or would it be more appropriate to create a custom event using the classic BOR approach?

Any advice, insights, or documentation references would be highly appreciated!

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: How to extend manage customer line items app in clean core way to add other standard logical fields
entry.content:
Hello, 

Another blocker of clean core way. 
We are trying to extend the manage customer line items app to have few more fields from other data sources . 


extending the i_arlineitem cds view with those fields  does not reflect in the data source extension of CFL to enable them In app. 

we created a custom app on top of i_arlineitem which is released C0 contract . Now the issue we are facing is in ATC check level 2 warning of “Usage of internal API” is coming and blocking this custom app way also . 

Can anyone please help in adding other data source field in this manage customer line app or help in fixing that atc check .

entry.title: No preview generated for RAP Application in ADT
entry.content:
App previewHello Gurus,

I can't see preview of my demo RAP application i created on ADT. 

After scouring the app in ADT and inspecting the preview, few places where i found the issue is:

1) during the $metadata?sap_client call available in the network section of the inspect element

2) SQL error "SQL code: 1024" occurred while accessing table "AGR_USERS", available in the Feed Reader section of ADT.

The question here is, are these the real causes SAP is not generating the preview? Or, is it due to the downtime for the backend btp instance ?

While searching for the solution, one user had suggested to delete the current btp instance and re-create it for a new region. Is this a valid workaround? If this is case, probably, i will have to re-create my app from ground-up....

So guys, any other suggestions?

Best Regards....

entry.title: Recommended naming convention for custom objects in ABAP Cloud
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am working with ABAP Cloud in the context of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, and I would like to understand the recommended naming convention for custom development objects.

In classic ABAP development, custom objects are usually created using the Z* or Y* namespace, or a registered customer namespace. However, in ABAP Cloud, I would like to know what the recommended approach is for naming custom artifacts such as:

- Packages
- Classes and interfaces
- CDS views / CDS view entities
- Behavior definitions and behavior implementations
- Service definitions and service bindings
- Metadata extensions

Is there an SAP-recommended naming convention or best practice for these objects in ABAP Cloud?

For example, should we continue using Z*/Y*, use a specific prefix per application/domain, or follow a different convention aligned with Clean Core and ABAP Cloud development guidelines?

Any official SAP documentation, examples, or practical recommendations would be very helpful.

Thank you.

entry.title: Issue dynamically hiding a column in an adaptation project
entry.content:
Hello,

I'm working on an adaptation project for a custom app I've developed in RAP with annotations. My requirement is to hide columns of a table created through annotations according to the value of virtual fields.

Unfortunately as I've seen in other posts that using a boolean virtual element with a LineItem.hidden(#virtelementname) doesn't work, so I've moved to an adaptation project. My approach is to read the virtual fields from the odata4 model through typescript, get the columns from the table through and hide them through setVisible. This is the function that I attach this to the event modelContextChange of the table (the columns are visible for some objects and invisible for others) in the code file generated by the adaptation editor.

	setColumnsVisible = async function (this: ControllerExtension, table: Table ) {
		const bindingContext = this.getView().getBindingContext();
		if (!bindingContext) {
			return;
		}
		const path = `${bindingContext.sPath}`;
		const context : Context = this.getView().getModel().bindContext(path);
		interface VisibilityResponse {Column1Hidden: boolean; };
		const res = await context.requestObject() as VisibilityResponse;
		const columns: Column[] = table.getColumns();
		const columnToHide = columns.find( curr => curr.getPropertyKey().includes("ColumnToHide") );
		columnToHide.setVisible(!res.Column1Hidden);
	};

The result of this is that when Column1Hidden is true the label of the column is hidden but the values are still there. My objective is to hide the whole column including the values.

As a sidenote, just for kicks I tried getting the index of the column, removing it from the table with removeColumn, setting visible to false (on the column) and inserting it back at the right index, it didn't work either. I also tried to call invalidate() on the Table and Column controls after setting the visibility (in case it's evaluated during rendering)  and still no luck.

Does anybody have any ideas how to achieve this?

Thanks,

Ben

entry.title: Error when trying to create a service entry sheet (SES) via i_serviceentrysheettp_2
entry.content:
Hello,

I am encountering an error when trying to create a new service entry sheet (SES) with a position and two cost centers in one position (account assignment).
In this case, there are 3 entities involved (header, item, and account assignment).

When I have more than 1 account assignment position, I am always getting the following error:
411 06  Sum of quantities >3,000< larger than total quantity

Although my quantities are okay - 2 in item and 1 + 1 in account assignment positions.

Code example:

METHOD if_oo_adt_classrun~main.

DATA:
ls_header_data TYPE i_serviceentrysheettp_2,
ls_item_data TYPE i_serviceentrysheetitemtp_2,
ls_acc_ass TYPE I_SesAccountAssignmentTP_2,

lt_header_crt TYPE TABLE FOR CREATE i_serviceentrysheettp_2\\serviceentrysheet,
lt_item_cba TYPE TABLE FOR CREATE i_serviceentrysheettp_2\\serviceentrysheet\_serviceentrysheetitem,
lt_acc_ass_cba TYPE TABLE FOR CREATE i_serviceentrysheettp_2\\ServiceEntrySheetItem\_AccountAssignment,
ls_acc_ass_cba LIKE LINE OF lt_acc_ass_cba,
lv_item_cid TYPE string,
lv_accass_cid TYPE string.

m_out = out.

ls_header_data-serviceentrysheetname = 'Demo SES'.
ls_header_data-purchaseorder = '4500001414'.
ls_header_data-sesoriginobjecttype = `EX`.
ls_header_data-PurgDocExternalReference = 'FA163E5F512FEFE199B52BA24E8069BE'.
ls_header_data-PostingDate = '20260527'.

APPEND INITIAL LINE TO lt_header_crt ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_hdr_crt>).
<ls_hdr_crt> = CORRESPONDING #( ls_header_data CHANGING CONTROL ).
<ls_hdr_crt>-%cid = `HEADER_1` .

APPEND INITIAL LINE TO lt_item_cba ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_itm_cba>).
<ls_itm_cba>-%cid_ref = 'HEADER_1'.

lv_item_cid = |Item_2|.
lv_accass_cid = |Accass_2|.

CLEAR: ls_item_data, ls_acc_ass_cba.
ls_item_data-purchaseorderitem = '00020'.
ls_item_data-confirmedquantity = '2.000'.
ls_item_data-accountassignmentcategory = 'K'.
ls_item_data-multipleacctassgmtdistribution = '1'.
ls_item_data-ServicePerformanceDate = '20260610'.
ls_item_data-ServicePerformanceEndDate = '20260610'.

APPEND INITIAL LINE TO <ls_itm_cba>-%target ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<item_data>).
<item_data> = CORRESPONDING #( ls_item_data CHANGING CONTROL ).
<item_data>-%cid = lv_item_cid. " 'Item_1'.

ls_acc_ass_cba-%cid_ref = lv_item_cid.
DATA(lv_accass_cid_2) = 'ACCASS_2_1'.

CLEAR ls_acc_ass.
ls_acc_ass-GLAccount = '0021351301'.
ls_acc_ass-CostCenter = '2000B11020'.
ls_acc_ass-MultipleAcctAssgmtDistrPercent = '50'.
ls_acc_ass-Quantity = '1.000'.

APPEND INITIAL LINE TO ls_acc_ass_cba-%target ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<accass_data>).
<accass_data> = CORRESPONDING #( ls_acc_ass CHANGING CONTROL ).
<accass_data>-%cid = lv_accass_cid_2.

lv_accass_cid_2 = 'ACCASS_2_2'.

CLEAR ls_acc_ass.
ls_acc_ass-GLAccount = '0021351301'.
ls_acc_ass-CostCenter = '2000B16110'.
ls_acc_ass-MultipleAcctAssgmtDistrPercent = '50'.
ls_acc_ass-Quantity = '1.000'.

APPEND INITIAL LINE TO ls_acc_ass_cba-%target ASSIGNING <accass_data>.
<accass_data> = CORRESPONDING #( ls_acc_ass CHANGING CONTROL ).
<accass_data>-%cid = lv_accass_cid_2. " 'Accass_1'.

APPEND ls_acc_ass_cba TO lt_acc_ass_cba.

MODIFY ENTITIES OF i_serviceentrysheettp_2
ENTITY serviceentrysheet
CREATE
FROM lt_header_crt
CREATE BY \_serviceentrysheetitem
FROM lt_item_cba
ENTITY ServiceEntrySheetItem
"CREATE
" FROM lt_item_cba
CREATE BY \_AccountAssignment
FROM lt_acc_ass_cba
FAILED DATA(ls_failed_crt)
REPORTED DATA(ls_reported_crt)
MAPPED DATA(ls_mapped_crt).

COMMIT ENTITIES
RESPONSE OF i_serviceentrysheettp_2
FAILED DATA(ls_failed_commit)
REPORTED DATA(ls_reported_commit).

 Maybe someone will see an error in my code and provide me a hint. Thank you.

entry.title: Use nextlink/skiptoken in RAP custom entity
entry.content:
Dear all,

is somebody aware of support in RAP for OData's nextlink/skiptoken feature in a custom entity? Ofc one would need to implement parts of it by oneself but I was wondering whether SAP provides an API to facilate the implementation.

Kind regards

Nick

entry.title: RAP Navigation
entry.content:
Hello All,
I had a scenario in which for my VBAP list report in RAP, the material column should be clickable,and when clicked upon should open the object page of that material. I searched the internet and found this may be possible #WITH_NAVIGATION_PATH,and tried implementing it in the following way.

 


The material field became clickable but nothing happens after that.

Its an RAP application , previewed through ADT(Odata V2 UI).

Any extra thing that i maybe missing.

Thanks in advance

entry.title: RAP Navigation
entry.content:
Hello All,

I had a scenario in which I had to make a column clickable in an RAP list report , and when clicked the control should be navigated to the object page of that column(In my case material).

I browsed the net and found that such functionality is maybe possible by #WITH_NAVIGATION_PATH.I implemented it in the following way

After this , the column indeed became clickable however nothing happens after the click


Anything which I am missing, any annotations, your help is very much appreciated.

Thanks in Advance

entry.title: RAP - Behavior projection - Error on activating Behavior projection
entry.content:
Hi All,  

Managed scenario - Created three levels of RAP BO.

Behavior definition is successfully activated however could not able to activate the "Behavior Projection".  Follwing is the error under ADT Problem - 

The association "_CUSTOMER" cannot be used as a transactional association since the target entity "ZSRA_R_CUSTTP" is not redirected to "ZSRA_C_CUST" in the CDS definition.

define root view entity  ZSRA_C_CUST
 provider contract transactional_query
  as projection on  ZSRA_CUSTTP
{
  key CustomerId,
      CustomerName,
      CreatedBy,
      CreatedAt,
      LastChangedBy,
      ChdLocalLastChangedAt,
      LastChangedAt,
      /* Assoziationen */
      _project : redirected to composition child ZSRA_C_PRJ
}

------------

define view entity ZSRA_C_PRJ
  as projection on ZSRA_R _PRJTP
{
  key CustomerId,
  key ProjectId,
      ProjectName,
      Project,
      ProjectType,
      Data,
      Exporter,
      CreatedBy,
      CreatedAt,
      LastChangedBy,
      PrjLocalLastChangedAt,
      LastChangedAt,
      /* Associations */
      _customer : redirected to parent /OSGMBH/ ZSRA_C_CUST,
      _sys: redirected to composition child ZSRA_C_SYS

}

 ------------
define view entity ZSRA_C_SYS as projection on ZSRA_ R_SYS
{
    key ProjectId,
key SystemId,
    key CustomerId,
    SystemName,
    Service,
    Tenant,
    CreatedBy,
    CreatedAt,
    LastChangedBy,
    SysLocalLastChangedAt,
    LastChangedAt,
    /* Associations */
    _project: redirected to parent  ZSRA_C_PRJ,
     _customer
}

Behavior Projection –

projection;
strict ( 2 );
use draft;
define behavior for ZSRA_C_CUST alias Customer
{
  use create;
  use update;
  use delete;
  use action Edit;
  use action Activate;
  use action Discard;
use action Resume;
  use action Prepare;
use association _project { create; with draft; }
}

define behavior for ZSRA_C_PRJ alias Project
{
  use update;
  use delete;
  use association _customer { with draft; }
  use association _sys { create; with draft; }
}
define behavior for ZSRA_C_SYS alias System
{
  use update;
  use delete;
  use association _project { with draft; }
  use association _customer { with draft; } <-- Error
}

Thanks & regards

Shobhan Rana

entry.title: On‑the‑Fly Excel Download in a RAP Fiori App — No Database Table, No Persistence, Pure CDS + ABAP
entry.content:
Author's note: Views are my own. Object names in this post are illustrative placeholders (ZC_*, ZCL_*) — adapt them to your own naming convention. The pattern is what matters, not the names.

Tags: ABAP RAP · CDS Custom Entity · Fiori Elements · Open XML / XLSX · cl_abap_zip · cl_salv_table · IF_RAP_QUERY_PROVIDER · ABAP Cloud

TL;DR

You can serve an Excel file from a Fiori Elements list without ever creating a database table for it. Combine a CDS Custom Entity (whose data is delivered by an ABAP class), a query provider that implements IF_RAP_QUERY_PROVIDER, and the standard @Semantics.largeObject annotation — and the binary you build at runtime is streamed straight to the user's browser as a .xlsx.

This blog shows the end‑to‑end pattern with two flavours of XLSX generation:

  1. Multi‑sheet workbook assembled by hand from Open XML parts using cl_abap_zip.
  2. Single‑sheet workbook generated in one line via cl_salv_table=>to_xml( c_type_xlsx ).

Both are 100% ABAP Cloud–compatible, both use only released APIs, and neither persists a single byte.

1. The problem

Every RAP project eventually hits this requirement:

"Put a Download button on the Fiori list page — when the user clicks, hand them an Excel file."

The default reflex is to:

  1. Create a Z‑table with a RAWSTRING column.
  2. Build the binary in a background job.
  3. Expose it via a transactional CDS view.
  4. Add a clean‑up job for stale rows.
  5. Transport the table, the data, and the cleanup job through every system.

That works — but it's wasteful when the file is derivable from master data. You're persisting something that goes stale the moment master data changes, you're transporting binaries, and you're paying storage for content you could regenerate in milliseconds.

There is a stateless alternative.

2. The pattern in one picture

Fiori Elements List Report
        │  GET …/ZC_TmplDownload?$filter=TemplateType eq 'TYPE_A'
        ▼
CDS Custom Entity   ZC_TmplDownload
   @ObjectModel.query.implementedBy: 'ABAP:ZCL_TMPL_DWNLD_PROVIDER'
        │
        ▼
ABAP Class   ZCL_TMPL_DWNLD_PROVIDER
   IF_RAP_QUERY_PROVIDER~SELECT
        │  builds XLSX as XSTRING in memory at request time
        ▼
   @Semantics.largeObject  ⇒  Browser "Save as…" dialog

No database table sits on this path. The binary lives in memory only as long as the HTTP request takes to complete.

3. The CDS Custom Entity

A custom entity is a CDS view whose data is not fetched by the SADL runtime — it is delivered by an ABAP class you write. That makes it the perfect anchor for runtime‑computed binaries.

@EndUserText.label: 'Template Download'
@ObjectModel.query.implementedBy: 'ABAP:ZCL_TMPL_DWNLD_PROVIDER'
define custom entity ZC_TmplDownload
{
      @UI.hidden: true
  key TemplateType : abap.char(20);

      @UI.lineItem: [{ position: 20, label: 'Filename' }]
      Filename     : abap.char(255);

      @UI.lineItem: [{ position: 30, label: 'Download' }]
      @Semantics.largeObject: {
            mimeType                    : 'MimeType',
            fileName                    : 'Filename',
            acceptableMimeTypes         : [
                  'application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet'
            ],
            contentDispositionPreference: #ATTACHMENT
      }
      Attachment   : abap.rawstring(0);

      @Semantics.mimeType: true
      @UI.hidden:   true
      MimeType     : abap.char(100);
}

What each piece does

Annotation / Field Why it matters

@ObjectModel.query.implementedBy: 'ABAP:ZCL_TMPL_DWNLD_PROVIDER'Tells the RAP runtime: don't query the DB — call this class.
key TemplateTypeLets the same entity ship N different files. We'll return one row per template type.
Attachment : abap.rawstring(0)The binary payload column. rawstring(0) = unbounded length.
@Semantics.largeObjectThe magic annotation. Fiori Elements renders this column as a Download link, fetches it lazily as a separate $value request, and pushes the bytes to the browser with the right MIME type and filename.
mimeType / fileName reference fieldsPer‑row file name and content type — each row downloads as its own correctly‑named .xlsx.
contentDispositionPreference: #ATTACHMENTForces the browser to save, not to render the file in‑tab.

💡 Tip: Even on download‑only entities, populate acceptableMimeTypes. Some Fiori Elements versions use it to harden the download link.

4. The query provider class — skeleton

CLASS zcl_tmpl_dwnld_provider DEFINITION
  PUBLIC FINAL CREATE PUBLIC.

  PUBLIC SECTION.
    INTERFACES if_rap_query_provider.

  PRIVATE SECTION.
    METHODS build_template_a
      RETURNING VALUE(rv_xlsx) TYPE xstring.
    METHODS build_template_b
      RETURNING VALUE(rv_xlsx) TYPE xstring.
ENDCLASS.

IF_RAP_QUERY_PROVIDER has a single method that matters here: SELECT. RAP calls it for every GET against the entity.

IF_RAP_QUERY_PROVIDER~SELECT — the dispatcher

METHOD if_rap_query_provider~select.
  DATA lt_result TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ZC_TmplDownload.

  DATA(lt_filter)    = io_request->get_filter( )->get_as_ranges( ).
  DATA(lt_requested) = io_request->get_requested_elements( ).

  " Optional pushdown: only build the file the user actually asked for.
  DATA(lv_wants_a) = abap_true.
  DATA(lv_wants_b) = abap_true.
  LOOP AT lt_filter INTO DATA(ls_filter) WHERE name = 'TEMPLATETYPE'.
    lv_wants_a = COND #( WHEN 'TYPE_A' IN ls_filter-range THEN abap_true ELSE abap_false ).
    lv_wants_b = COND #( WHEN 'TYPE_B' IN ls_filter-range THEN abap_true ELSE abap_false ).
  ENDLOOP.

  IF lv_wants_a = abap_true.
    APPEND VALUE #(
      templatetype = 'TYPE_A'
      filename     = 'TemplateA.xlsx'
      attachment   = build_template_a( )
      mimetype     = 'application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet'
    ) TO lt_result.
  ENDIF.

  IF lv_wants_b = abap_true.
    APPEND VALUE #(
      templatetype = 'TYPE_B'
      filename     = 'TemplateB.xlsx'
      attachment   = build_template_b( )
      mimetype     = 'application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet'
    ) TO lt_result.
  ENDIF.

  io_response->set_total_number_of_records( lines( lt_result ) ).
  io_response->set_data( lt_result ).
ENDMETHOD.

Two ideas worth flagging:

  1. The XLSX is generated at every GET. That is the whole point — the file is always fresh.
  2. Filter pushdown is honoured manually. io_request->get_filter( )->get_as_ranges( ) returns the OData $filter as ABAP ranges. Use it to decide which template to build, so a click on row A never computes row B.

5. Technique A — Hand‑build a multi‑sheet XLSX with cl_abap_zip

An .xlsx file is a ZIP archive of XML files that follow the Open XML SpreadsheetML specification. If you control the XML, you control the file — no SAP Office, no DOI, no OLE, and no third‑party library.

5.1 The minimum parts you need

TemplateA.xlsx (zip)
├── [Content_Types].xml          ← MIME map for every part inside
├── _rels/.rels                  ← root relationship → workbook.xml
└── xl/
    ├── workbook.xml             ← declares the sheets
    ├── styles.xml               ← fonts / borders / cellXfs (we use a "bold header" xf)
    ├── _rels/
    │   └── workbook.xml.rels    ← workbook → sheet1..N + styles
    └── worksheets/
        ├── sheet1.xml
        ├── sheet2.xml
        ├── sheet3.xml
        └── sheet4.xml

5.2 Build the package skeleton

DATA: lo_zip      TYPE REF TO cl_abap_zip,
      lv_xml      TYPE string,
      lv_xstr     TYPE xstring,
      lv_zip_xstr TYPE xstring.

lo_zip = NEW cl_abap_zip( ).

Each XML part is built as a string, converted to UTF‑8 xstring, then dropped into the zip.

5.3 [Content_Types].xml — the MIME map

lv_xml =
  `<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>` &&
  `<Types xmlns="http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/package/2006/content-types">` &&
    `<Default Extension="rels" ContentType="application/vnd.openxmlformats-package.relationships+xml"/>` &&
    `<Default Extension="xml" ContentType="application/xml"/>` &&
    `<Override PartName="/xl/workbook.xml"`
        && ` ContentType="application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet.main+xml"/>` &&
    `<Override PartName="/xl/worksheets/sheet1.xml"`
        && ` ContentType="application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.worksheet+xml"/>` &&
    `<Override PartName="/xl/worksheets/sheet2.xml"`
        && ` ContentType="application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.worksheet+xml"/>` &&
    `<Override PartName="/xl/worksheets/sheet3.xml"`
        && ` ContentType="application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.worksheet+xml"/>` &&
    `<Override PartName="/xl/worksheets/sheet4.xml"`
        && ` ContentType="application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.worksheet+xml"/>` &&
    `<Override PartName="/xl/styles.xml"`
        && ` ContentType="application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.styles+xml"/>` &&
  `</Types>`.

lv_xstr = cl_abap_codepage=>convert_to( source = lv_xml codepage = 'UTF-8' ).
lo_zip->add( name = '[Content_Types].xml' content = lv_xstr ).

5.4 The root relationship

lv_xml =
  `<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>` &&
  `<Relationships xmlns="http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/package/2006/relationships">` &&
    `<Relationship Id="rId1"`
       && ` Type="http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/officeDocument/2006/relationships/officeDocument"`
       && ` Target="xl/workbook.xml"/>` &&
  `</Relationships>`.

lv_xstr = cl_abap_codepage=>convert_to( source = lv_xml codepage = 'UTF-8' ).
lo_zip->add( name = '_rels/.rels' content = lv_xstr ).

5.5 workbook.xml — wire up four sheets

lv_xml =
  `<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>` &&
  `<workbook xmlns="http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/spreadsheetml/2006/main"`
     && ` xmlns:r="http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/officeDocument/2006/relationships">` &&
    `<sheets>` &&
      `<sheet name="Header"      sheetId="1" r:id="rId1"/>` &&
      `<sheet name="Header_Text" sheetId="2" r:id="rId2"/>` &&
      `<sheet name="Item"        sheetId="3" r:id="rId3"/>` &&
      `<sheet name="Item_Text"   sheetId="4" r:id="rId4"/>` &&
    `</sheets>` &&
  `</workbook>`.

Each r:id resolves through xl/_rels/workbook.xml.rels to a real worksheets/sheetN.xml file. Get this wrong and Excel refuses the file with the dreaded "We found a problem with some content" dialog.

5.6 The workbook's relationships and styles

" xl/_rels/workbook.xml.rels — workbook → sheets + styles
lv_xml =
  `<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>` &&
  `<Relationships xmlns="http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/package/2006/relationships">` &&
    `<Relationship Id="rId1" Type=".../worksheet" Target="worksheets/sheet1.xml"/>` &&
    `<Relationship Id="rId2" Type=".../worksheet" Target="worksheets/sheet2.xml"/>` &&
    `<Relationship Id="rId3" Type=".../worksheet" Target="worksheets/sheet3.xml"/>` &&
    `<Relationship Id="rId4" Type=".../worksheet" Target="worksheets/sheet4.xml"/>` &&
    `<Relationship Id="rId5" Type=".../styles"    Target="styles.xml"/>` &&
  `</Relationships>`.
" (Type URLs abbreviated for readability — use the full openxmlformats.org URLs in real code.)
" xl/styles.xml — xf 0 = normal, xf 1 = bold (used by header rows via s="1")
lv_xml =
  `<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>` &&
  `<styleSheet xmlns="http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/spreadsheetml/2006/main">` &&
    `<fonts count="2">` &&
      `<font><sz val="11"/><name val="Calibri"/></font>` &&
      `<font><b/><sz val="11"/><name val="Calibri"/></font>` &&
    `</fonts>` &&
    `<fills count="2">` &&
      `<fill><patternFill patternType="none"/></fill>` &&
      `<fill><patternFill patternType="gray125"/></fill>` &&
    `</fills>` &&
    `<borders count="1">` &&
      `<border><left/><right/><top/><bottom/><diagonal/></border>` &&
    `</borders>` &&
    `<cellStyleXfs count="1">` &&
      `<xf numFmtId="0" fontId="0" fillId="0" borderId="0"/>` &&
    `</cellStyleXfs>` &&
    `<cellXfs count="2">` &&
      `<xf numFmtId="0" fontId="0" fillId="0" borderId="0" xfId="0"/>` &&
      `<xf numFmtId="0" fontId="1" fillId="0" borderId="0" xfId="0"/>` &&
    `</cellXfs>` &&
  `</styleSheet>`.

5.7 A worksheet — bold header + data row

" Sheet 1 — Header  (KEY_FIELD | GROUP_FIELD | STATUS)
" s="1"          → cellXfs index 1 (bold) defined in styles.xml
" t="inlineStr"  → string lives inside the cell (no shared-strings table needed)
lv_xml =
  `<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>` &&
  `<worksheet xmlns="http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/spreadsheetml/2006/main">` &&
    `<sheetData>` &&
      `<row r="1">` &&
        `<c r="A1" t="inlineStr" s="1"><is><t>Key Field</t></is></c>` &&
        `<c r="B1" t="inlineStr" s="1"><is><t>Group Field</t></is></c>` &&
        `<c r="C1" t="inlineStr" s="1"><is><t>Status</t></is></c>` &&
      `</row>` &&
      `<row r="2">` &&
        `<c r="A2" t="inlineStr"><is><t>KEY01</t></is></c>` &&
        `<c r="B2" t="inlineStr"><is><t>GRP01</t></is></c>` &&
        `<c r="C2" t="inlineStr"><is><t>X</t></is></c>` &&
      `</row>` &&
    `</sheetData>` &&
  `</worksheet>`.

Two pragmatic tricks worth knowing:

  • t="inlineStr" — store strings inside the cell. The alternative is a shared‑strings part, more efficient for large repeated text but it doubles the moving parts. For a download template, inline is the right trade‑off.
  • s="1" — refer to cellXfs index 1, which we defined as bold. Headers come out bold without further effort.

5.8 Finalize

lo_zip->save( RECEIVING zip = lv_zip_xstr ).
rv_xlsx = lv_zip_xstr.   "  ← this xstring IS the .xlsx file

That xstring flows up through set_data( ) into the Attachment column of the custom entity, and the @Semantics.largeObject runtime serves it to the browser as a real .xlsx.

6. Technique B — Single‑sheet XLSX via cl_salv_table (the easy route)

When you only need one worksheet, skip the manual XML and let SALV do it:

METHOD build_template_b.
  TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_row,
           field_a TYPE char20,
           field_b TYPE char10,
           field_c TYPE char10,
         END OF ty_row.

  DATA lt_data    TYPE TABLE OF ty_row.
  DATA lo_table   TYPE REF TO cl_salv_table.
  DATA lo_columns TYPE REF TO cl_salv_columns_table.
  DATA lo_column  TYPE REF TO cl_salv_column.

  lt_data = VALUE #(
    ( field_a = 'SAMPLE_KEY_001' field_b = 'CODE01' field_c = 'GRP01' ) ).

  TRY.
      cl_salv_table=>factory(
        IMPORTING r_salv_table = lo_table
        CHANGING  t_table      = lt_data ).

      lo_columns = lo_table->get_columns( ).

      TRY.
          lo_column = lo_columns->get_column( 'FIELD_A' ).
          lo_column->set_long_text( 'Field A' ).
          lo_column->set_output_length( 20 ).
        CATCH cx_salv_not_found.
      ENDTRY.

      " ★ The single line that turns a SALV table into a complete .xlsx xstring
      rv_xlsx = lo_table->to_xml( xml_type = if_salv_bs_xml=>c_type_xlsx ).

    CATCH cx_salv_msg.
      RETURN.
  ENDTRY.
ENDMETHOD.

cl_salv_table=>to_xml( c_type_xlsx ) returns a fully‑formed XLSX xstring. The catch: it produces exactly one sheet. That is why the multi‑sheet template needs Technique A.

7. Wire it up in Fiori Elements

In your service definition:

@EndUserText.label: 'Template Downloads'
define service ZUI_TEMPLATE_DOWNLOADS {
  expose ZC_TmplDownload;
}

Bind it to a service binding (OData V4 → UI), generate a Fiori Elements List Report, and you'll see one row per template type, each with a download icon in the Download column. Click → the browser receives TemplateA.xlsx or TemplateB.xlsx directly from memory.

That's it. Zero DB tables. Zero persistence.

8. Why this pattern is worth adopting

Concern Stateless Custom Entity (this blog) Persisted "blob in a Z‑table"

FreshnessAlways live — built per requestGoes stale; needs a refresh job
StorageNoneRAWSTRING in DB, every release
Transport / cutoverJust codeCode and data
AuthorizationEnforced in the classSame, plus DB‑level checks
TestabilityPlain ABAP unit test on the helper methodsNeeds DB fixtures
PerformanceOne ZIP build per clickOne DB read — but you paid for the build earlier, in a job

For templates and other derivable artifacts the trade is one‑sided in favour of statelessness. Reach for the persisted variant only when (a) the file is genuinely expensive to build (think tens of MB / millions of rows) and (b) it doesn't need to reflect the latest master data.

9. Pitfalls (so you don't have to learn them the hard way)

  1. UTF‑8 everywhere. cl_abap_codepage=>convert_to( … codepage = 'UTF-8' ) for every XML part. Excel will reject mixed encodings.
  2. Relationship IDs (rId…) must match across workbook.xml and xl/_rels/workbook.xml.rels. Easiest way to corrupt the file.
  3. Don't forget styles.xml in BOTH [Content_Types].xml AND workbook.xml.rels. If you reference s="1" without a registered styles part, Excel opens with a "repair" prompt.
  4. @Semantics.largeObject requires mimeType and fileName to point at columns — not literal strings. The annotation references field names.
  5. Don't mix shared strings with inline strings unless you really need to. t="inlineStr" keeps each sheet self‑contained.
  6. Filter pushdown costs nothing. Build only the row Fiori asked for — your download click should never compute the other template.
  7. Avoid stacks/classes that aren't released for ABAP Cloud (some XLSX helper classes aren't). cl_abap_zip, cl_abap_codepage, and cl_salv_table are the safe choices.
  8. Authorization checks belong in the class. A custom entity has no built‑in DB‑level access controls — your SELECT is the gatekeeper. Call your authorization object before set_data( ).
  9. Watch the response size. @Semantics.largeObject streams, but the gateway still buffers — keep templates under a few MB. For very large files, switch to a dedicated streaming endpoint.

10. Closing

The combination of CDS Custom Entity + IF_RAP_QUERY_PROVIDER + @Semantics.largeObject is one of the most under‑used corners of RAP. Once you internalise that the Attachment column is just an xstring you build at runtime, every "give me a download" requirement becomes a one‑class problem — no schema change, no transport drama, no stale binaries.

The same wiring works for:

  • PDF previews — build the bytes with the form runtime and serve them.
  • CSV exports — return UTF‑8 text (with BOM if Excel users will open them).
  • ZIP archives of multiple files — keep add( )‑ing into cl_abap_zip.
  • Image thumbnails — computed from master data.

Same plumbing. Different xstring.

If this pattern saved you a Z‑table, drop a 👍 and tell me what you used it for in the comments.

Happy RAP‑ing. 🛠️

Personal disclaimer

The views and techniques expressed here are my own and do not represent those of my employer or SAP. Code samples are illustrative — validate them in your own development system before adopting them in productive code.

entry.title: SAP PO 7.5 REST Receiver - Capture HTTP 500 response in Asynchronous Interface and send via alert
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I have an asynchronous interface in SAP PO 7.5 from SAP to D365(proxy to rest) using the REST Receiver Adapter.

When the target REST API (D365) returns HTTP 500, the response contains a useful JSON error like this:

{
"Message": "Receipt processing failed for Purchase Order "5*********"
}
I can see this response in the Message Monitor under the server response, but it is not available as the XI message payload.

My requirement is to automatically send this business error to users by email instead of manually copying it from Message Monitor.

My questions are:

Can SAP PO capture the HTTP 500 response body in a standard asynchronous REST receiver?
Can the REST Result Handler be used to extract the Message field and use it for email notifications?
If not, what is the SAP-recommended approach?

entry.title: Issue in activating ZDMO_RAP_GENERATOR in ABAP Cloud
entry.content:
In Cloud ABAP RAP I have pulled the abapgit files from cloud-abap-rap/README.md at main · SAP-samples/cloud-abap-rap · GitHub.

However, when I try to activate the ZDMO_RAP_GENERATOR I have errors in activation.

Can someone provide a solution to activate the ZDMO_RAP_GENERATOR.

entry.title: How to make specific fields read-only in an Action Parameter Popup (Abstract Entity) - ABAP 8.16
entry.content:
​I am working in an ABAP Release 8.16 environment using the RAP (ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model).

​I have a requirement where a custom action button on the UI triggers a popup dialog. This popup uses a custom Abstract Entity to define its input parameter fields.

​My Requirement:

When the user clicks the action button and the custom abstract entity popup appears, only the "Quantity" field should be editable. Every other field in that structure needs to be read-only.

​Here is a simplified version of my Abstract Entity:

@EndUserText.label: 'Action Parameter Popup'

define abstract entity ZC_MyActionParameter

{

  @EndUserText.label: 'Material ID'

  MaterialID : matnr;

  

  @EndUserText.label: 'Desired Quantity'

  Quantity : kwmeng;

  

  @EndUserText.label: 'Plant'

  Plant : werks_d;

}

 

And here is how it's defined in my Behavior Definition (BDEF):

action MyCustomAction parameter ZC_MyActionParameter result [1] $self;

Currently tried creating bdef for abstract entity and make feild readonly but not working 

And try to use object readonly UI annotation but is not available in this release

entry.title: How to use privileged Mode in a RAP Cross-BO Scenario for reading instance Features
entry.content:
Hi everybody, 

We have created in SAP BTP ABAP Environment two RAP Business Object with the following properties

  • A business Object ZAK_CommentTP
    • Having an authorization context NoCheckWhenPrivileged
    • with privileged mode disabling NoCheckWhenPrivileged;
    • The key field is called CommentId
  • A business Object ZAK_MainTP 
    • enabled for Cross-BO Scenarios source resolved, e.g. there is a field CommentId in the entity.

What we want to achieve is that authorization checks are performed for reading, creating the comments in ZAK_CommentTP.

However, if a user is authorized for an instance of  ZAK_MainTP , then he can read and create comments in ZAK_CommentTP which refer to this instance without being explicitly authorized for  the ZAK_CommentTP RAP BO.

To achieve this, we have created actions and functions in the behavior of ZAK_MainTP, which then call the corresponding EML statements in ZAK_CommentTP in Privileged Mode.

This works well for creating comments. Here, we have created an action  createComment in ZAK_MainTP  which uses the operation create-by-association for ZAK_CommentTP and the EML Statement

MODIFY ENTITIES OF ZAK_CommentTP PRIVILEGED

      ENTITY CommentHeader

       CREATE BY \_CommentChild ….

For Reading the Comments we have created a function which then uses Read Entities OF ZAK_CommentTP PRIVILEGED.

 

Now, we would like to know when a comment can be deleted. Therefore we can use the EML Statement  

    GET PERMISSIONS PRIVILEGED ONLY INSTANCE FEATURES OF ZAK_CommentTP        ENTITY ….

to get the result of the feature control in ZAK_CommentTP .      

The result of this EML Statement is of type structure for permissions result ZAK_CommentTP   .

However, there is no possibility of creating a function in ZAK_MainTP  which has this as result structure.        

 

So, I am searching for a way to create a function in the behavior for ZAK_MainTP whose  parameters are the same as for the  get_instance_features method in the ZAK_CommentTP  .

 

Any help and suggestions are welcome

entry.title: Repeated RAP update of business partner is causing error
entry.content:
Hi All,

I'm trying to update tax numbers of business partners using RAP. It is working if there is a single update. If multiple updates I'm trying each with its own commit then the following error is coming.

Parameter IV_X_SAVE is '' for FM BUPA_CENTRAL_CHANGE. It should be 'A'.

Anyone faced this error. Any solution for this.

Aravind

entry.title: In RAP how to filter an association via button
entry.content:
Hello All,

I do not find any answer online.  So I hope you can help me out.
I got a RAP app which contains of some CDS Views in parent child relation and some "normal" associations.
Each Entity has its own UUID and a reference to the root entity.
This root UUID is used to associate to a foreign CDS and all entries are displayed within all entities.
I now want one button which takes the UUID of the specific entity and filters the data of the association accordingly
A short coding example

define root table entity rootEntity
{
  key key  : key;
      uuid : uui;

      @ObjectModel               : {
        sort.enabled             : false,
        filter.enabled           : false
      }
      _Child : composition of exact one to many children;
     
      @ObjectModel               : {
       sort.enabled              : false,
       filter.enabled            : false
      }
      _foreign : association of exact one to many foreigns on $projection.uuid = _Activities.uuid_root_reference;
}

 Child

define table entity child
{
  key parent_key : key;
  key key        : key;
      uuid       : /uuid;

      @ObjectModel:{
        sort.enabled: false,
        filter.enabled: false
      }
      _parent : association to parent parent on $projection.parent_key = parent.key;
      
}

 on Consumption level

root

define root view entity c_parent
  provider contract transactional_query
  as projection on parent

{
  key     key   as Key,
          uuid  as UUID,
          @ObjectModel:{
             sort.enabled: false,
             filter.enabled: false
           }
          _Child : redirected to composition child c_children;

          @ObjectModel:{
            sort.enabled: false,
            filter.enabled: false
          }
          _foreign : redirected to c_foreign

}

 child

define view entity c_children
  as projection on children

  association of exact one to many c_foreigns as _foreigns on $projection.parentUUID = _foreigns.UUIDRootReference

{
  key key as Key,
  key parent_key as ParentKey,
      _parent.uuid as parentUUID,
      uuid,
         
      _parent : redirected to parent  c_parent,
      _foreigns
  
}

in parent and child bdef I now want to have this:

 action (features : instance) filterByUUID result [0..*] entity c_foreigns;
side effects {
    action filterByUUID affects entity _foreigns;
  }

The foreigns association is linked as line item reference and does not contain a filterbar.

{
        id                : 'Foreigns',
        purpose           : #STANDARD,
        position          : 10,
        label             : 'Foreigns',
        type              :  #LINEITEM_REFERENCE,
        targetElement     : '_foreigns'
      },

 In coding i want somethind like this:

READ ENTITIES OF parent IN LOCAL MODE
      ENTITY parent
      FIELDS ( uuid )
      WITH CORRESPONDING #( keys )
      RESULT DATA(lt_parents).

        LOOP AT keys ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_key>).

          LOOP AT lt_parents REFERENCE INTO DATA(lr_parent).

            SELECT
              *

            FROM
              foreigns

            WITH PRIVILEGED ACCESS

            WHERE
              uuid_root_reference = @LR_parent->uuid

            APPENDING TO TABLE
              @result.


          ENDLOOP.

      ENDLOOP.

 any hint how to achieve that I can filter the foreigns for the current entities uuid would be nice.
Thank you in advance and best regards.

Matthias
